name: Allen Dulles
role: CIA Director 1953–1961; architect of Guatemala coup and Bay of Pigs
quote: We had a success in Guatemala. We must not let Cuba become another Guatemala.

name: Richard Helms
role: CIA Director 1966–1973; authorized Chile operations; convicted of lying to Congress
quote: I have found no evidence that the CIA or any other US government agency sought to bring about a coup in Chile in September 1973.

name: William Colby
role: CIA Director 1973–1976; disclosed 'Family Jewels'; cooperated with Church Committee
quote: The CIA's involvement in assassination plots is not something that the United States government should be proud of.

name: Jacobo Árbenz
role: President of Guatemala 1951–1954; overthrown by CIA Operation PBSUCCESS
quote: I was not alone. The people of Guatemala and all the anti-imperialist peoples of the world struggle with me.

name: Salvador Allende
role: President of Chile 1970–1973; overthrown and killed in CIA-backed coup
quote: History is ours, and people make history. These are my last words, and I am certain that the seed which we have planted in the good conscience of thousands and thousands of Chileans will not be shaken.

name: Augusto Pinochet
role: Chilean dictator 1973–1990; arrested in London 1998; died under house arrest 2006
quote: I'm not a dictator. It's just that I have a grumpy face.

name: Henry Kissinger
role: National Security Adviser / Secretary of State; architect of Chile policy; Nobel Peace Prize 1973
quote: I don't see why we need to stand by and watch a country go communist due to the irresponsibility of its own people.

name: Dwight D. Eisenhower
role: US President 1953–1961; authorized Guatemala coup (PBSUCCESS) and planned Bay of Pigs
quote: I have authorized the Department of State and the Department of Defense to plan the establishment of the Cuban exile government.

name: John F. Kennedy
role: US President 1961–1963; Bay of Pigs failure; authorized Operation Mongoose
quote: How could I have been so stupid, to let them go ahead?

name: Lyndon B. Johnson
role: US President 1963–1969; authorized Brazil coup support (1964) and Dominican Republic invasion (1965)
quote: I sure don't want to wake up tomorrow morning and find out that Castro's taken over in the Dominican Republic.

name: Richard Nixon
role: US President 1969–1974; ordered CIA to 'make the economy scream' in Chile
quote: Make the economy scream in Chile to prevent Allende from coming to power or to unseat him.

name: Ronald Reagan
role: US President 1981–1989; authorized Contra war, Grenada invasion, backed El Salvador death squads
quote: The Contras are the moral equivalent of our Founding Fathers.

name: Fidel Castro
role: Prime Minister/President of Cuba 1959–2008; survived 600+ CIA assassination attempts
quote: The United States has no right to enforce an economic blockade against Cuba, a blockade that affects the Cuban people and constitutes an international crime.

name: Ernesto 'Che' Guevara
role: Cuban revolutionary; executed by CIA-backed Bolivian forces 1967
quote: The duty of every revolutionary is to make the revolution.

name: Daniel Ortega
role: Sandinista leader; survived Contra war; turned authoritarian as president from 2007
quote: We have shown that socialism is possible. Nicaragua is proof that imperialism can be defeated.

name: Manuel Noriega
role: Panamanian dictator; CIA asset 20+ years; captured in US invasion 1989; died in prison 2017
quote: The US created me, nurtured me, and when I stopped being useful, they decided to destroy me.

name: Hugo Chávez
role: President of Venezuela 1999–2013; survived US-backed coup 2002; confronted US hegemony
quote: The devil came here yesterday, right here. It smells of sulfur still today. Yesterday, ladies and gentlemen, from this rostrum, the president of the United States, the gentleman to whom I refer as the devil, came here.

name: Anastasio Somoza García
role: Nicaraguan dictator 1936–1956; created with US support after Marine occupation
quote: Yes, he may be a son of a bitch, but he is our son of a bitch. [attributed to FDR]

name: Jean-Bertrand Aristide
role: Haitian president; overthrown 1991, restored 1994, removed 2004 in US-facilitated ouster
quote: I was kidnapped. Let that be on the record. I was put in a plane that the Americans controlled.

name: Efraín Ríos Montt
role: Guatemalan dictator 1982–1983; convicted of genocide 2013 (overturned); US-backed with military aid
quote: We have no scorched earth policy. We have a policy of scorched communists.

name: Orlando Letelier
role: Chilean Foreign Minister under Allende; DINA assassinated him in Washington DC, 1976
quote: Neoliberal economics and political repression are not contradictions but two sides of the same system.

name: Archbishop Óscar Romero
role: Archbishop of San Salvador; assassinated March 1980 by US-linked death squads
quote: I would like to make a special appeal to the men of the army...in the name of God, in the name of this suffering people whose cries rise to heaven more loudly each day, I implore you, I beg you, I order you: Stop the repression.

name: Maj. Gen. Smedley Butler
role: USMC General; two-time Medal of Honor; whistleblower who exposed corporate nature of US interventions
quote: I spent 33 years in the Marines...and during that period I spent most of my time as a high-class muscle man for Big Business, for Wall Street and for the bankers.

name: Manuel Zelaya
role: Honduran president; ousted in 2009 coup; Obama administration did not restore him
quote: What happened on June 28, 2009 in Honduras was a coup d'état orchestrated by the Honduran oligarchy with the support of the United States.

name: Oliver North
role: NSC staffer who ran Iran-Contra scheme; shredded documents; later pardoned
quote: I didn't think I was doing anything wrong. I was told what to do, and I did it.

name: Evo Morales
role: Bolivia's first indigenous president 2006–2019; ousted amid election controversy
quote: What happened in Bolivia is a civic, Christian, democratic coup. The United States is behind all this.

name: Félix Rodríguez
role: CIA officer; captured and interrogated Che Guevara; later Iran-Contra operative
quote: I told Che that I had done everything I could for him...he died like a man.

name: Carlos Castillo Armas
role: CIA-installed Guatemalan president 1954–1957; reversed land reform; assassinated 1957
quote: The liberation movement is the work of the Guatemalan people, who have freed themselves from the communist menace.

name: George H.W. Bush
role: CIA Director 1976–1977; Vice President during Iran-Contra; ordered Panama invasion 1989
quote: This is not a military operation for the sake of imposing anything. We'll be in, we'll get our people out, and we'll be out.
